title: Select Teachings from the Gospel of John

yaml.title: Select Teachings from the Gospel of John

markdown.markdown:
```{=html}

Course Outline

Preamble: Teaching Style and Philosophy
1. Introduction
2. Jesus as God (1–3, 14)
3. Jesus in Creation
4. Jesus as Life and Light of Men
5. Jesus in Salvation — New Birth
6. Jesus: Grace and Truth vs. Moses and the Law
7. Jesus Baptizes with the Holy Spirit
8. Jesus: The Only Way to the Father
9. Jesus' Priestly Prayer
``` ```{=html}

Preamble: Teaching Style and Philosophy

Encourage questions and discussion
Uses John as a starting point, but explores themes across all of Scripture
Prioritizes depth over coverage—no expectation to finish all material
Materials provided to registered participants
``` ```{=html}

I. Introduction

A. Overview — Watch Video
1. Bible Project — The Book of John
a. bibleproject.com/guides/book-of-john/
B. The Book
1. Authorship and Date
a. Church Fathers (unanimous): John the Apostle, written in Ephesus, late 1st century
— Irenaeus (c. 180 AD): "John, the disciple of the Lord… did himself publish a Gospel during his residence at Ephesus"
— Clement of Alexandria (c. 215 AD): John wrote a "spiritual Gospel" after the Synoptics
b. Modern Scholarship: Anonymous; product of a Johannine community; c. 90–100 AD
— The "Beloved Disciple" is the eyewitness source but the final author is debated
— Earliest manuscript fragment (P52) dated c. 125–150 AD sets the upper limit
2. Stated Purpose
a. John 20:31 — "…but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name."
3. Distinctives of John
a. ~80–90% of content is unique — not found in Matthew, Mark, or Luke
b. Seven "I AM" Statements (John 6:35; 8:12; 10:7,9; 10:11,14; 11:25; 14:6; 15:1,5)
c. Seven Signs (John 2; 4; 5; 6:1–14; 6:16–21; 9; 11)
d. Seven Titles of Jesus
C. About the Author
1. Life of John
a. Born c. AD 6–15, Galilee — son of Zebedee and Salome
b. Called among first disciples; inner circle with Peter and James
c. The "Beloved Disciple" — remained at the crucifixion; entrusted with Mary's care
d. Pillar of the Jerusalem church; founded churches across Asia Minor
e. Exiled to Patmos under Domitian (c. AD 95) — wrote Revelation there
f. Died naturally in Ephesus c. AD 100 — only apostle not martyred
2. Christian Tradition
a. Attributed authorship: Gospel of John, 1–3 John, Revelation
b. Known for teaching: "Little children, love one another" (Jerome)
c. Disciple chain: John → Polycarp → Irenaeus — key line of apostolic tradition
``` ```{=html}

II. Jesus as God

A. Seed of the Woman
1. Old Testament Prophecy
a. Genesis 3:15 — "he shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel"
2. New Testament Fulfillments
a. Galatians 4:4 — "God sent forth his Son, born of woman…"
b. Romans 16:20 — "The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet"
c. Hebrews 2:14 — "…through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death…"
d. Revelation 12:9, 17 — the ancient serpent; war on her offspring
B. Seed of Abraham
1. Old Testament Prophecy
a. Genesis 22:18 — "in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed"
b. See also Genesis 12:3; 17:19
2. New Testament Fulfillments
a. Galatians 3:16 — Paul identifies Jesus as the singular seed
b. Galatians 3:29 — believers share the promise through union with Christ
c. Matthew 1:1 — "the son of David, the son of Abraham"
d. Luke 3:34 — physical descent from Abraham affirmed
C. Old Testament Prophecies of a Divine Messiah
1. Isaiah 9:6 — "Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace"
2. Isaiah 7:14 — "the virgin shall conceive… Immanuel" (God with us)
3. Micah 5:2 — ruler from Bethlehem "whose origin is from of old, from ancient days"
4. Jeremiah 23:5–6 — "The Lord [YHWH] is our righteousness"
D. Melchizedek — Type of Christ's Eternal Priesthood
1. Old Testament
a. Genesis 14:18–20 — king-priest receives tithes from Abraham
b. Psalm 110:4 — "You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek"
2. New Testament Application (Hebrews)
a. Hebrews 7:1–3 — "having neither beginning of days nor end of life…"
b. Hebrews 7:15–17 — Jesus arises in the likeness of Melchizedek
c. Hebrews 7:24–25 — "he holds his priesthood permanently… able to save to the uttermost"
E. The "Son of Man" — Divine Ruler (Daniel 7)
1. Old Testament
a. Daniel 7:13–14 — given everlasting dominion over all peoples and nations
2. New Testament — Jesus Claims This Title
a. Matthew 26:64 — "you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven"
F. Jesus' Seven "I AM" Statements in John
1. John 6:35 — "I am the bread of life"
2. John 8:12 — "I am the light of the world"
3. John 10:9 — "I am the door"
4. John 10:11 — "I am the good shepherd"
5. John 11:25 — "I am the resurrection and the life"
6. John 14:6 — "I am the way, and the truth, and the life"
7. John 15:1 — "I am the true vine"
G. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus' Deity or Worshiping Him
1. John 20:28 — Thomas: "My Lord and my God!" (clearest verbal affirmation)
2. Matthew 16:16 — Peter: "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God"
3. Matthew 14:33 — Disciples worship after walking on water: "Truly you are the Son of God"
4. John 1:41 — Andrew: "We have found the Messiah"
5. Matthew 2:11 — The Magi fall down and worship the infant Jesus
6. Matthew 28:9 — Women at the resurrection take hold of his feet and worship him
7. Matthew 28:17 — The eleven disciples worship at the Great Commission
8. John 9:38 — Man born blind: "Lord, I believe." And he worshiped him
H. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus as the Christ / Messiah
1. John 1:41 — Andrew to Peter: "We have found the Messiah"
2. Mark 8:29 — Peter at Caesarea Philippi: "You are the Christ"
3. Matthew 16:16 — Peter (fuller): "the Christ, the Son of the living God"
4. Luke 9:20 — Peter: "The Christ of God"
5. John 11:27 — Martha: "you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is coming into the world"
6. John 4:29, 42 — Samaritan woman and villagers: "the Savior of the world"
7. Matthew 14:33 — Disciples: "Truly you are the Son of God"
8. Mark 15:39 — Centurion at the cross: "Truly this man was the Son of God!"
I. Jesus' Pre-Existence Claims (primarily in John)
1. John 8:58 — "before Abraham was, I am"
2. John 17:5 — "the glory that I had with you before the world existed"
3. John 17:24 — "you loved me before the foundation of the world"
4. John 6:38 — "I have come down from heaven"
5. John 6:62 — "the Son of Man ascending to where he was before"
6. John 16:28 — "I came from the Father… going to the Father"
7. John 8:23 — "I am from above… I am not of this world"
8. John 3:13 — "he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man"
J. Additional New Testament Declarations of Jesus as God
1. Colossians 1:17 — "he is before all things, and in him all things hold together"
2. Colossians 2:9 — "the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily"
3. Philippians 2:5–6 — "in the form of God… equality with God"
4. Hebrews 1:8 — Father speaks of Son: "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever"
5. Romans 9:5 — "the Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever"
6. Titus 2:13 — "our great God and Savior Jesus Christ"
7. Philippians 2:10–11 — "every knee should bow… every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord" (Isaiah 45:23 applied to Jesus)
``` ```{=html}

III. Jesus in Creation

(Content to be developed)
``` ```{=html}

IV. Jesus as Life and Light of Men

(Content to be developed)
``` ```{=html}

V. Jesus in Salvation — New Birth

(Content to be developed)
``` ```{=html}

VI. Jesus: Grace and Truth vs. Moses and the Law

(Content to be developed)
``` ```{=html}

VII. Jesus Baptizes with the Holy Spirit

(Content to be developed)
``` ```{=html}

VIII. Jesus: The Only Way to the Father

(Content to be developed)
``` ```{=html}

IX. Jesus' Priestly Prayer

(Content to be developed)
```
